improvements_list.description: State × Event Table for §1.1 spec lifecycle state machine — complete 6×6 table with all cells filled (transitions, REJECTs, no-transitions), satisfying INV-010 self-conformance
Gate 7 test protocol — concrete 6-step operational procedure with specific pass criteria, cross-referencing SPEC-BENCH-002; resolves Gate 7 lacking a concrete measurement method
Meta-standard proportional weight guide — separate table for meta-standards (PART 0: 45-60%) with explicit DO NOT instruction, resolving the self-referential proportionality paradox
Assembly algorithm complexity analysis — O(T) per module, O(M×T) full rebuild, with worked example (scheduler: 3,450 lines), satisfying INV-004 for assembly
Cascade protocol complexity analysis — O(I×M) identification, O(A×V) re-validation, with edge case note about non-recursive cascading, satisfying INV-004 for cascade
Expanded verification prompt in §5.6 — from 6 items to 16 items organized into Positive checks (12), Negative checks (3), Integration check (1) covering all INV-001 through INV-020
Authoring sequence with explicit dependency edges — each of 16 steps now states which prior steps it depends on and why, preventing LLM reordering
Negative specification §4.1 — DO NOT copy-paste executive summary as full formal model; prevents common LLM shortcut of treating summaries as complete definitions
Negative specification §6.1.1 — DO NOT define decision spikes without explicit time budgets; prevents LLMs from treating spikes as full implementation tasks
Negative specification §6.1.4 — DO NOT present deliverable order as flat list without dependency edges; prevents LLMs from implementing in list order rather than dependency order
Named benchmark IDs (SPEC-BENCH-001 through SPEC-BENCH-004) with expanded measurement descriptions and explicit design point paragraph
Seven new glossary entries: Assembly script, Blast radius, Context budget, Cross-cutting module, Invariant registry, Reasoning reserve, Signal-to-noise ratio — resolving INV-009 gaps
Document Structure template reorganization — Non-Negotiables/Non-Goals nested under §0.1 with section numbering flexibility note in §0.3
Cross-reference additions — preamble step 5 adds §10.1/§10.2 references; ordering rationale adds §0.4 ring architecture cross-ref
Resolved Open Question 1 — machine-readable cross-references resolved with regex patterns for §X.Y, INV-NNN, ADR-NNN tokens
Deferred Open Question 6 — composability deferred with candidate [SpecName]:INV-NNN syntax and concrete revisit criteria

invariant_violations.description: Most algorithms now have complexity analysis, but the consistency validation checks (CHECK-1 through CHECK-9) lack individual worked examples showing pass and fail cases
PART I remains at 3.8% of document (120 lines) — technically within the meta-standard 3-6% range but at the floor; some content could be expanded to better serve the formal model

gate_failures.description: No empirical validation performed — no competent engineer has confirmed spec readiness by implementing from it without clarifying questions
Test protocol now defined (improvement) but not yet executed — no LLM implementation test has been run against representative chapters

remaining_gaps.description: PART I disproportionately thin (120 lines, 3.8%) — at bottom of meta-standard range; formal model, state machines, and complexity analysis could be more developed
Empirical Gate 6/7 validation never performed — the spec prescribes measurement (now with a protocol) but has never been measured
Open Questions 2, 4, 5 remain fully unaddressed — multi-document specs, formal verification bridge, and confidence levels
Meta-overhead question unresolved — 3,182-line meta-standard for writing ~2,000-line domain specs; the standard may be approaching diminishing returns on additions
Some PART II element specification chapters lack sufficient worked examples showing before/after of element authoring (e.g., Chapter 7 testing elements, Chapter 8 voice guide)
Cross-reference to §5.5 comparison blocks added in §5.1 item 2 but comparison block element itself (§5.5) is lightly specified — could benefit from a worked example

rationale: Iteration 1 delivers 16 substantive improvements across all three evaluation dimensions with zero regressions. The P0 self-conformance fixes (state × event table, Gate 7 protocol, meta-standard proportions, algorithm complexity analysis) directly address the spec's most visible self-bootstrapping failures. The LLM-effectiveness improvements (3 new negative specs, authoring dependency DAG, expanded 16-item verification prompt) are well-targeted at real LLM failure modes. However, the spec has not yet converged: PART I remains underdeveloped, Gates 6-7 lack empirical validation, three open questions are unaddressed, and the meta-overhead concern (a 3,182-line standard) deserves structural attention. The quality score of 88 (Good) with clear P1 remaining gaps means further iteration should yield meaningful improvement. Recommend focusing next iteration on: (1) expanding PART I foundations with richer formal model content, (2) adding worked examples to element specification chapters that lack them, and (3) addressing at least one of the remaining open questions (confidence levels being the most tractable).
